info.name: Order Lookup Debug — ReturnProMax
info.description:
Tests every Shopify order lookup strategy to diagnose why order resolution fails for Fynd orders.

## Setup
1. Set `shop_domain` (e.g. fynd-store-1.myshopify.com)
2. Set `access_token` from the Session table in your database
3. Set `order_name` to the order you're testing (e.g. FYNDSHOPIFYX14126)
4. Set `app_url` to your deployed app URL (e.g. https://returnpromax-1.onrender.com)

## Flow
The app resolves Fynd orders via this chain:
1. GraphQL `name:"#FYNDSHOPIFYX14126"` (quoted with #)
2. GraphQL `name:"FYNDSHOPIFYX14126"` (quoted without #)
3. GraphQL `name:#FYNDSHOPIFYX14126` (unquoted with #)
4. GraphQL `name:FYNDSHOPIFYX14126` (unquoted without #)
5. GraphQL general search `"#FYNDSHOPIFYX14126"` (no name: prefix)
6. REST API `GET /orders.json?name=%23FYNDSHOPIFYX14126` (exact match)
7. Pagination scan of recent 1250 orders
8. Metafield search `metafields.$app.fynd_order_id:"..."`
9. source_identifier search

item.name: 0. App Debug Endpoint (all strategies at once)

request.description: Runs ALL lookup strategies from within the app (uses the app's own Shopify session). This is the easiest way to diagnose — just open this URL while logged into the Shopify admin.
Query: name:"#FYNDSHOPIFYX14126" — quoted value with # prefix
Query: name:"FYNDSHOPIFYX14126" — quoted value without # prefix
Query: FYNDSHOPIFYX14126 — general text search, no name: field prefix

item.name: 6. REST API: GET orders.json?name=%23FYNDSHOPIFYX14126 (exact match with #)

request.description: REST API exact name filter: name=#FYNDSHOPIFYX14126 (URL-encoded as %23). This is the most reliable method — exact match, no search tokenization.

item.name: 7. REST API: GET orders.json?name=FYNDSHOPIFYX14126 (exact match without #)

request.description: REST API exact name filter without # prefix
Lists the 50 most recent orders. Check if FYNDSHOPIFYX14126 appears in the list. If not, the order may be too old for the pagination scan.

item.name: 9. Direct GID lookup (once you have the ID)

request.description: Direct O(1) lookup by GID — use the numeric ID from the REST API result. This ALWAYS works if the order exists and you have the correct ID.
